title: 1563rd meeting (June 2026) (DH) - Rule 9.1 - Communication from the applicant (20/03/2026) in the AD Osiguritelna Polisa Skopje and Others group of cases v. North Macedonia (Application No. 62544/19) [anglais uniquement]
text: SECRETARIAT / SECRÉTARIAT SECRETARIAT OF THE COMMITTEE OF MINISTERS SECRÉTARIAT DU COMITÉ DES MINISTRES Contact: Ireneusz Kondak Tel: 03.90.21.59.86 Date: 20/03/2026 DH-DD(2026)380 Document distributed under the sole responsibility of its author, without prejudice to the legal or political position of the Committee of Ministers. Meeting: 1563rd meeting (June 2026) (DH) Communication from the applicant (20/03/2026) in the AD Osiguritelna Polisa Skopje and Others group of cases v. North Macedonia (Application No. 62544/19). Information made available under Rule 9.1 of the Rules of the Committee of Ministers for the supervision of the execution of judgments and of the terms of friendly settlements. * * * * * * * * * * * Document distribué sous la seule responsabilité de son auteur, sans préjuger de la position juridique ou politique du Comité des Ministres. Réunion : 1563e réunion (juin 2026) (DH) Communication du requérant (20/03/2026) relative au groupe d’affaires AD Osiguritelna Polisa Skopje et autres c. Macédoine du Nord (requête n° 62544/19) [anglais uniquement] Informations mises à disposition en vertu de la Règle 9.1 des Règles du Comité des Ministres pour la surveillance de l’exécution des arrêts et des termes des règlements amiables. DH-DD(2026)380: Rule 9.1: Communication from the applicant in AD Osiguritelna Polisa Skopje and Others v. North Macedonia. Document distributed under the sole responsibility of its author, without prejudice to the legal or political position of the Committee of Ministers. 88 BOGDANOV & BOGDANOV !.•\\', FJRM Coun2il of Europe Director~te,Gen7ral Hum an Rights and Rule of Law Department for the Execution of Judgments of the ECHR 67075, Strasbourg- CEDEX France Subject: Rule 9 communication concerning the execution of the judgment of the European Court of Hu man Rights in the case of AD Osiguritelna Po/isa and others v. North Macedonia (App.No.62544/19 and 6430/20) in the part of the friendly settlement declarations signed by the applicants and the Government Dear Madam/ Sir, As a legal representative of all of the applicants: AD Osiguritelna Polisa Skopje, Croatia Osiguruvanje AD Skopje (fully owned by Croatia Osiguranje dd Zagreb, Croatia), - Triglav Osiguruvanje AD Skopje (owned by Triglav Group d.d., Ljuljana, Slovenia), Macedonia Skopje- Vienna Insu rance Group (owned by Vienna lnsurance Group, Wien, Austria), Eurolink Osiguruvanje AD Skopje (fully owned by the GRAWE Group, Graz, Austria) - JS Argo DOOEL Skopje, - Tehnokar Sasho DOOEL Skopje and Granit AD Skopje in the case of AD Osiguriteln a Polisa and others v. North Macedonia (App.No.62544/19 and 6430/20), 1 respectfully submit the following communication, with reference to Rule 9, point 1 of the Ru les of the Com mittee of Ministers for the supervision of the execution of judgments and of the terms of friendly settlements. The applicants are lega l persons incorporated in North Macedonia in the field of insurance and construction works. Four of the applicants are fully, or in majority, owned by their parent companies established in Croatia, Slovenia and Austria. ln 2019 and 2020, the applicants raised complaints before the Court that they have been a victim of a violation of Article 6 of the Convention, and of Article 1 of Protocol No. 1 to the Convention due to the. non-enforcement of 149 domestic final judgments ordering a State-owned company to pay the applicant companies various amounts of money. On 15 May 2023, within the framework of the non-contentious phase before the Court, the parties signed friendly settlement declarations in which t r e applicant companies agreed ro waive any further daims 9 6yJJ.napnnaHCt010APeA 11 6p. .1'1a, Te11,/ a1<c: 023178912 1000 C1conjt', l\·1aKt'A0t111ja @ www.bogdanov.mk 1 DH-DD(2026)380: Rule 9.1: Communication from the applicant in AD Osiguritelna Polisa Skopje and Others v. North Macedonia. Document distributed under the sole responsibility of its author, without prejudice to the legal or political position of the Committee of Ministers. 88 BOGDANOV & BOGDANOV LAW FIRM against the respondent State in respect of the domestic judgments giving rise to the applications. Furthermore, the applicants agreed, within one month following the notification of the Court's decision striking the respective part of the case out of its list of cases, to secure a fresh notification by the bailiffs that no payment has been executed by the debtor in the meantime- between the date the declarations were signed, until the date of the Court's decisions becomes final. ln addition, the applicant companies undertook to submit to the Government, within one month following the notification of the Court's decision striking the respective part of the cease out of its list of cases, formai notification issued by the bailiffs that the domestic enforcement proceedings of the 149 final judgments subject matter of the friendly settlement declarations, have been withdrawn and terminated by the applicants. On their part, the State undertook an obligation, within four months from the date of notification of the Court's decision (that is until 18 March 2026), to pay the applicant companies various amounts of money. The total amount of money that the State pledged by the declarations to pay to the applicants is 117.007.160 denars- in national currency, or approximately 1.902.555 euros. The amount is equivalent to the amou nt owned by the debtor as of 15 May 2023 (the date when the declarations were signed) calculated according to the domestic courts' orders issued in ail of the 149 final judgments. The Court' s judgment striking the applications out of its list of cases, in accordance with Article 39 of the Convention, in respect of the complaints covered by the friendly settlement declarations became final on 18 November 2025. The applicants, on their part,· discharged in full their obligations undertaken with the friendly settlement declarations. Within one month, they terminated ail of the 149 domestic enforcement proceedings against the State-owned debtor, and, at the same time, they provided the Government with official note from the bailiffs attesting that no payment has been received from the debtor until the date the enforcement proceedings were formally terminated. On the other hand, the State did not discharge its obligation to pay the agreed amounts of money within the settled period of 4 months. Therefore, on behalf of the applicant companies, 1 would like to inform the Department for the Execution of Judgments of the European Court of Human Rights that the respondent State did not honor its monetary obligation undertaken by itself with the FS Declarations, and its obligation undertaken by Article 46 of the Convention. The FS Declarations and the Court's judgment now rem ain unexecuted, in the same manner as the unexecuted domestic monetary judgments that were subject matter of the friendly declarations, and that were pending for execution for more than 15 years before the domestic bailiffs. The applicants would like to draw the attention of the Department that, at the moment, after the Court' s judgment became final, they are in a more vulnerable position sin ce the domestic enforcement proceedings for ail of the 149 domestic judgments have been terminated on the applicants' request, and now ail of them aretime bared to be reinstated aga in. Accordingly, there is no default interest running neither for the State-owned company, neither for the State- in default of their obligations. The applicants are left again in total uncertainty whether and when the State will honor its monetary obligations deriving from the terminated enforcement proceedings, and now 9 6y11. case_name: CASE OF AD OSIGURITELNA POLISA SKOPJE AND OTHERS v. NORTH MACEDONIA
